title: veronica hederifolia subsp. triloba
intro: Veronica hederifolia subsp. triloba, also known as the Trilobe Speedwell or Wood Speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Veronica hederifolia subsp. triloba include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, and its leaves can also be dried and made into a powder for later use.
